read_plan.preview: **[[Ascendant Path]]** — [[Auralis]]'s dominant religion. Institutionally organized, politically integrated with the Council, runs schools and officiates life ceremonies. Core doctrine: Flux mastery is spiritual advancem

The traditions are not mutually exclusive in practice. A farming family in rural Terravelle may practice the Covenant, maintain Ancestral Current customs around their grandparents' graves, and have some vague awareness o

Religion in Solumora does not live primarily in formal theology. It lives in what people actually do: the rituals maintained in [[Household Religious Practice|households]], the tensions that surface in [[Workplace Religi

The numeric tier is the spell's **Flux handling requirement**. Each tier is roughly an order-of-magnitude jump in difficulty, capacity, stability, and precision. Every few tiers feels like a categorically different class
_Barely any Flux. The threshold most people never bother to formally test._
_A small, usable trickle. The first tier where deliberate spellwork is practical._
_The world average. The first tier where magic becomes genuinely useful in daily life._

Auralis society leans toward a soft caste structure, where social standing is loosely tied to magical ability. This is not a rigid hierarchy of law — high-tier casters do not hold formal political office by virtue of the

Auralis embraces the full spectrum of [[Discipline|Disciplines]], including those that make other cultures uncomfortable. Experimentation with Mind and Soul disciplines is not openly banned and in certain scholarly or mi

The principal settlements of Auralis are [[Solhaven]] (the capital, built up coastal terraces on the western shore) and [[Emberfall]] (the interior research and agricultural center). Auralis has several additional port c
Solhaven approaches a million residents and is the most vertically stratified city in Solumora. Its terrace zones function as distinct social worlds stacked on each other.

Terravelle society values stability and efficiency above all. Where other cultures may chase magical prestige, Terravelle embraces the philosophy that the best spell is the one that accomplishes the task with the least e

Low to mid-tier casting is common and unremarkable. [[Grimoires]] here tend to favor Triangle and Square sigil shapes — fast, safe, reproducible, and affordable in [[Flux]]. [[Discipline|Disciplines]] with predictable fa

[[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] share a complicated relationship shaped by fundamentally opposed philosophies. Where Auralis pursues magical innovation and supremacy, Terravelle prizes restraint and function. The [[Equato

The principal settlements of Terravelle are [[Valdenmoor]] (the capital, in the central river valley), [[Ashford]] (the primary eastern port), and [[Greyveil]] (the northern waypoint at the edge of the settled belt). Doz

Current [[Flux]] research is dominated by institutional networks in both kingdoms, each organized around distinct philosophical priorities and strategic orientations. **Contemporary military preparation is increasingly r

**[[Crestward Flux Institute]]** pursues cost reduction, parallel execution patterns, and methods to achieve higher-tier effects through coordinated low-tier practitioner teams. Leadership: [[Casten Miel]] ([[Control Tie

**[[Northern Terravelle Academy]]** emphasizes practical settlement-scale application and coordinated research on [[Rift Incursions|rift mechanics]]. The Academy operates independently of state control (through funding d

**Independent scholars** operate throughout both kingdoms, conducting collaborative research that neither kingdom prioritizes internally. [[Kedn Rast]] ([[Control Tier|T3]]) exemplifies this institutional model, conducti

Guild certification follows a standard three-stage progression: apprenticeship, journeyman work, and certification assessment. The stages vary in length by trade and in difficulty by the tier grade being sought.

Most trades have multiple certification grades tied to [[Control Tier]] capability. The grades are trade-specific rather than universal: a T3 certification in structural sigil application means something different from a

Guild documentation requirements are not optional conditions on certification — they are themselves part of what is being certified. A practitioner who can perform the work but cannot maintain accurate field records duri

Guild membership in [[Terravelle]] carries weight beyond trade certification. The guilds provide dispute resolution, wage standards, seasonal assistance funds, apprenticeship placement, housing recommendations, and the i

Wolfpoint was founded by the Voss family — a lineage of soldier scholars who had spent careers inscribing [[Sigils]] onto armour, blades, and field equipment, and grown deeply frustrated with what the sigil system could

Wolfpoint retains its military heritage in everything. The settlement is organized around discipline and function — buildings are low and wind-resistant, layouts are practical, nothing is built for display. The Voss line
Wolfpoint knows [[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] exist. It trades with both, maintains occasional correspondence, and has no interest in being absorbed by either.

What Wolfpoint has built over seven generations is not a finished system. [[Hypertext]] is still being developed — new symbol sequences are being tested, failure modes are being catalogued, and the limits of what a linea

Emberfall's research identity depends on field systems that are less visible than laboratories but equally decisive. Council-backed desert work now runs through a practical chain: permitting and compliance at [[Emberfall

A scholar at [[Emberfall]] commits themselves to mastery of one thing. Some choose a specific [[Sigils|sigil]] — they will spend years refining it, expanding its applications, understanding its boundaries in ways that in

Emberfall scholars do not conduct pure research in the way theoretical mages might. Their work is immediately applied. A scholar working on a combat sigil conducts experiments in controlled environments, documents result

[[Emberfall]] is led by a director appointed (in practice) by whoever on the [[The Council of Auralis|Council]] has strongest interest in [[Flux]] advancement — currently [[Drest]] and the Culmination Faction. The direct

Surface ruins only. Broken foundations, partially buried structural elements, fragments of inscription on materials of unknown composition. The buildings, where they can be reconstructed from their remnants, were large a

No migration evidence. No record of where the population went. No graves. The absence of bodies is considered by most researchers to be the most significant single fact about the ruins — a civilization this large and thi

Among the dozens of ruin sites catalogued across the desert, three are categorically different from the rest. They are not simply broken-down structures — they are areas where the [[Flux]] field itself behaves abnormally
**Location:** Eleven days south of [[Halveth]], ~4 degrees north latitude.

The Path holds that Flux is not merely a force to be used. It is the world's underlying structure made accessible — the medium through which reality can be engaged at its deepest level. A person who masters Flux does not

The Ascendant Path's formal priesthood are called Luminants. They are all mid-tier casters at minimum — the Path considers it theologically incoherent for its clergy to lack meaningful Flux capability. In practice, most
The Path's official position on T0s is one of its more carefully worded doctrines. The Path does not condemn non-casters. Everyone is on the path. Tier is where you begin, not what you are.

For most Auralis residents, the Ascendant Path is not theology. It is culture. The ceremonies are attended. The Luminants are respected. The general framework — Flux mastery matters, advancement is good, your tier is a m

The [[Sigils|sigil]] system, for all its power, is bounded by what a shape can express. A Circle has six outer points. That means six variables, defined once, firing in one configuration. It cannot natively represent bra
Hypertext is written as a linear sequence of symbols, read left to right. Each symbol in the sequence serves one of three roles:

Hypertext can be taught and learned. It requires no special innate ability beyond what any [[Flux Users|Formulist]] already has — the aptitude for Hypertext is the same aptitude for sigil use, applied differently. What i

[[Wolfpoint]] is the only known center of Hypertext development. Situated in the far north, well beyond the cultural reach of either [[Terravelle]] or [[Auralis]], it operates outside both kingdoms' magical traditions. T
